prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a widely used framework for modeling stock prices. Key parameters were estimated as follows: the current price is $663.02; the time horizon is 20 trading days (approximately 0.079365 years); an annualized drift (expected return) of 9% (0.09) was assumed, reflecting long-term average growth; and an annualized volatility of 18% (0.18) was used, representing the historical fluctuation of LMT. The point estimate is calculated as S_0 * exp(mu * t). Confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution of future prices, using standard deviations based on the assumed volatility and time horizon, along with corresponding Z-scores for each confidence level.
